NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to Joel son of Pethuel:
text.Rotherham: The word of Yahweh, which came unto Joel, son of Pethuel.

text.NRSV: Hear this, O elders, give ear, all inhabitants of the land! Has such a thing happened in your days, or in the days of your ancestors?
text.Rotherham: Hear this, ye elders, and give ear, all ye inhabitants of the land,—hath this ever happened in your days? or in the days of your fathers?

text.NRSV: Tell your children of it, and let your children tell their children, and their children another generation.
text.Rotherham: Concerning it, to your children, tell ye the story,—and your children, to their children, and their children, to the generation following:—

text.NRSV: What the cutting locust left, the swarming locust has eaten. What the swarming locust left, the hopping locust has eaten, and what the hopping locust left, the destroying locust has eaten.
text.Rotherham: That which was left by the creeping locust, hath the swarming locust eaten, and, that which was left by the swarming locust, hath the grass locust eaten; and, that which was left by the grass locust, hath the corn locust eaten.

text.NRSV: Wake up, you drunkards, and weep; and wail, all you wine-drinkers, over the sweet wine, for it is cut off from your mouth.
text.Rotherham: Awake, ye drunkards, and weep, and howl, all ye drinkers of wine,—over the new wine, because it hath been cut off from your mouth.

text.NRSV: For a nation has invaded my land, powerful and innumerable; its teeth are lions’ teeth, and it has the fangs of a lioness.
text.Rotherham: For, a nation, hath come up over my land, bold, and without number,—his teeth, are the teeth of a lion, and, the fangs of a lioness, hath he!

text.NRSV: It has laid waste my vines, and splintered my fig trees; it has stripped off their bark and thrown it down; their branches have turned white.
text.Rotherham: He hath turned my vine to a waste, and my fig-tree to splinters,—he hath, barked it clean, and cast it down, bleached are its branches.

text.NRSV: Lament like a virgin dressed in sackcloth for the husband of her youth.
text.Rotherham: Wail thou, like a virgin girded with sackcloth, for the owner of her youth.

text.NRSV: The grain offering and the drink offering are cut off from the house of the Lord. The priests mourn, the ministers of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cut off are the meal-offering and the drink-offering, from the house of Yahweh,—In grief are the priests, the attendants on Yahweh:—

text.NRSV: The fields are devastated, the ground mourns; for the grain is destroyed, the wine dries up, the oil fails.
text.Rotherham: Laid waste is the field, in grief is the soil,—for laid waste is the corn, abashed is the new wine, languisheth the oil.

text.NRSV: Be dismayed, you farmers, wail, you vinedressers, over the wheat and the barley; for the crops of the field are ruined.
text.Rotherham: Turn pale, ye husbandmen, howl, ye vinedressers, over the wheat, and over the barley,—for perished is the harvest of the field.

text.NRSV: The vine withers, the fig tree droops. Pomegranate, palm, and apple—all the trees of the field are dried up; surely, joy withers away among the people.
text.Rotherham: The vine, is abashed, and, the fig-tree, languisheth,—pomegranate, palm also, and apple—all the trees of the field, have withered, Yea abashed is gladness, away from the sons of men.

text.NRSV: Put on sackcloth and lament, you priests; wail, you ministers of the altar. Come, pass the night in sackcloth, you ministers of my God! Grain offering and drink offering are withheld from the house of your God.
text.Rotherham: Gird yourselves and beat the breast—ye priests, howl, ye attendants of the altar, go in and wrap yourselves for the night in sackcloth, ye attendants on my God,—for, withholden from the house of your God, are the meal-offering and the drink- offering.

text.NRSV: Sanctify a fast, call a solemn assembly. Gather the elders and all the inhabitants of the land to the house of the Lord your God, and cry out to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Hallow ye a fast, Call a solemn assembly, Gather, O elders, all the inhabitants of the land, unto the house of Yahweh your God,—and make ye outcry unto Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Alas for the day! For the day of the Lord is near, and as destruction from the Almighty it comes.
text.Rotherham: Alas for the day! For near is the day of Yahweh, and, as a veritable Destruction from the Destroyer, shall it come.

text.NRSV: Is not the food cut off before our eyes, joy and gladness from the house of our God?
text.Rotherham: Is it not, before our eyes, that, food, hath been cut off? From the house of our God, rejoicing and exultation.

text.NRSV: The seed shrivels under the clods, the storehouses are desolate; the granaries are ruined because the grain has failed.
text.Rotherham: Rotted hath the seed, under their clods, Laid waste are their stores, Thrown down are the garners,—Yea abashed is the corn.

text.NRSV: How the animals groan! The herds of cattle wander about because there is no pasture for them; even the flocks of sheep are dazed.
text.Rotherham: How do the beasts groan! Perplexed are the herds of oxen, because there is no pasture for them,—even, the flocks of sheep, are destroyed!

text.NRSV: To you, O Lord, I cry. For fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness, and flames have burned all the trees of the field.
text.Rotherham: Unto thee, O Yahweh, will I cry,—for, a fire, hath consumed the pastures of the wilderness, and, a flame, hath set ablaze all the trees of the field.

text.NRSV: Even the wild animals cry to you because the watercourses are dried up, and fire has devoured the pastures of the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Even the beasts of the field, moan unto thee,—because dried up are the channels of water, and, a fire, hath devoured the pastures of the wilderness.

text.NRSV: Blow the trumpet in Zion; sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let all the inhabitants of the land tremble, for the day of the Lord is coming, it is near—
text.Rotherham: Blow ye a horn in Zion, sound an alarm in my holy mountain, let all the inhabitants of the land, tremble,—for coming is the day of Yahweh, for it is near!—

text.NRSV: a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness! Like blackness spread upon the mountains a great and powerful army comes; their like has never been from of old, nor will be again after them in ages to come.
text.Rotherham: A day of obscurity and deep gloom, a day of cloud, and thick darkness, as dusk, spread over the mountains,—a people, many and bold, like whom, hath not been from age-past times, and, after whom, shall not be again unto the years of generation after generation.

text.NRSV: Fire devours in front of them, and behind them a flame burns. Before them the land is like the garden of Eden, but after them a desolate wilderness, and nothing escapes them.
text.Rotherham: Before him, hath a fire, devoured, and, after him, shall a flame, consume,—As the garden of Eden, is the land before him, but, after him, a desert most desolate, Moreover also, escape, giveth he none.

text.NRSV: They have the appearance of horses, and like war-horses they charge.
text.Rotherham: As the appearance of horses, is his appearance, and, as war-horses, so, shall they run:

text.NRSV: As with the rumble of chariots, they leap on the tops of the mountains, like the crackling of a flame of fire devouring the stubble, like a powerful army drawn up for battle.
text.Rotherham: Like the noise of chariots on the tops of the mountains, shall they rattle along, like the noise of a flame of fire, devouring dry straw,—like a people bold, arrayed for battle.

text.NRSV: Before them peoples are in anguish, all faces grow pale.
text.Rotherham: Because of him, shall peoples’, be in anguish,—all faces, have withdrawn their colour.

text.NRSV: Like warriors they charge, like soldiers they scale the wall. Each keeps to its own course, they do not swerve from their paths.
text.Rotherham: Like heroes, shall they run, like men of war, shall they mount a wall,—and, every one—along his own road, shall they march along, and shall not change their paths;

text.NRSV: They do not jostle one another, each keeps to its own track; they burst through the weapons and are not halted.
text.Rotherham: Nor, against each other, shall they strike, Each—on his own highway, shall they march,—though, in among the weapons, they fall, they shall not stop.

text.NRSV: They rush upon the city, they run upon the wall; they climb up into the houses, they enter through the windows like a thief.
text.Rotherham: Upon the city, shall they leap, on the wall, shall they run, up the houses, shall they climb,—through the windows, shall they enter, like a thief,

text.NRSV: The earth quakes before them, the heavens tremble. The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.
text.Rotherham: Before him, hath quaked the earth, have trembled the heavens,—the sun and the moon, have become dark, and, the stars, have withdrawn their shining;

text.NRSV: The Lord utters his voice at the head of his army; how vast is his host! Numberless are those who obey his command. Truly the day of the Lord is great; terrible indeed—who can endure it?
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, hath uttered his voice, before his host, for great indeed is his camp, for bold is he who executeth his word,—for great is the day of Yahweh, and awful exceedingly, Who then shall endure it?

text.NRSV: Yet even now, says the Lord, return to me with all your heart, with fasting, with weeping, and with mourning;
text.Rotherham: Even now, therefore, urgeth Yahweh, Turn ye unto me, with all your heart,—and with fasting and with weeping, and with lamentation;

text.NRSV: rend your hearts and not your clothing. Return to the Lord, your God, for he is gracious and merciful, slow to anger, and abounding in steadfast love, and relents from punishing.
text.Rotherham: And rend your heart, and not your garments, turn therefore, unto Yahweh your God,—for, gracious and full of compassion, is he, slow to anger, and abundant in loving- kindness, and will grieve over calamity.

text.NRSV: Who knows whether he will not turn and relent, and leave a blessing behind him, a grain offering and a drink offering for the Lord, your God?
text.Rotherham: Who knoweth, he may turn and grieve,—and leave behind him, a blessing, a meal-offering and a drink-offering, to Yahweh your God?

text.NRSV: Blow the trumpet in Zion; sanctify a fast; call a solemn assembly;
text.Rotherham: Blow ye a horn, in Zion,—hallow a fast, call a solemn assembly:

text.NRSV: gather the people. Sanctify the congregation; assemble the aged; gather the children, even infants at the breast. Let the bridegroom leave his room, and the bride her canopy.
text.Rotherham: Gather the people, hallow a convocation, collect the elders, gather the children, and the sucklings of the breasts,—let the bridegroom, come forth, from his chamber, and the bride from her bower:

text.NRSV: Between the vestibule and the altar let the priests, the ministers of the Lord, weep. Let them say, “Spare your people, O Lord, and do not make your heritage a mockery, a byword among the nations. Why should it be said among the peoples, ‘Where is their God?’ ”
text.Rotherham: Between the porch and the altar, let the priests, weep, the attendants of Yahweh,—and let them say—Look with pity, O Yahweh, upon thy people, and do not deliver thine inheritance to reproach, that the nations, should mock them, Why should they say among the peoples, Where is their God?

text.NRSV: Then the Lord became jealous for his land, and had pity on his people.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh became jealous for his land,—and took pity on his people;

text.NRSV: In response to his people the Lord said: I am sending you grain, wine, and oil, and you will be satisfied; and I will no more make you a mockery among the nations.
text.Rotherham: Then answered Yahweh, and said to his people:—Behold me! sending you the corn, and the new wine and the oil, so shall ye be satisfied therewith; and I will not make you, any more, a reproach among the nations.

text.NRSV: I will remove the northern army far from you, and drive it into a parched and desolate land, its front into the eastern sea, and its rear into the western sea; its stench and foul smell will rise up, for he has done great things.
text.Rotherham: And, the Northerner, will I remove far from you, and drive him into a land parched and desolate, with, his face, toward the eastern sea, and, his rear, toward the hinder sea,—then shall come up his ill odour, yea his stench, shall ascend, because he hath shown himself great in doing.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, O soil; be glad and rejoice, for the Lord has done great things!
text.Rotherham: Be not thou afraid, O soil,—exult and rejoice, because Yahweh, hath shown himself great, in doing.

text.NRSV: Do not fear, you animals of the field, for the pastures of the wilderness are green; the tree bears its fruit, the fig tree and vine give their full yield.
text.Rotherham: Be not afraid, ye beasts of the field, for sprouted have the pastures of the wilderness,—for, the tree, hath borne its fruit, the fig-tree and the vine, have yielded their wealth.

text.NRSV: O children of Zion, be glad and rejoice in the Lord your God; for he has given the early rain for your vindication, he has poured down for you abundant rain, the early and the latter rain, as before.
text.Rotherham: Ye sons of Zion, then, exult and be glad in Yahweh your God, for he hath given you the seed-rain, in right manner,—Yea he hath caused to descend for you a down-pour, of seed-rain and of the harvest-rain in the first month;

text.NRSV: The threshing floors shall be full of grain, the vats shall overflow with wine and oil.
text.Rotherham: So shall the threshing-floors, be filled, with corn,—and the vats, overflow, with new wine and oil.

text.NRSV: I will repay you for the years that the swarming locust has eaten, the hopper, the destroyer, and the cutter, my great army, which I sent against you.
text.Rotherham: Then will I make good to you the years which were eaten by the swarming locust, the grass locust, and the corn locust and the creeping locust,—even my great army, which I sent among you.

text.NRSV: You shall eat in plenty and be satisfied, and praise the name of the Lord your God, who has dealt wondrously with you. And my people shall never again be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall eat and eat, and be satisfied, and shall praise the name of Yahweh your God, who hath dealt with you wondrously,—so shall my people, not be abashed, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: You shall know that I am in the midst of Israel, and that I, the Lord, am your God and there is no other. And my people shall never again be put to shame.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, in the midst of Israel, I am, and that, I, Yahweh, am your God, and none else,—and my people, shall not be abashed, unto times age-abiding.

text.NRSV: Then afterward I will pour out my spirit on all flesh; your sons and your daughters shall prophesy, your old men shall dream dreams, and your young men shall see visions.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, afterwards, I will pour out my spirit upon all flesh, and your sons and your daughters, shall prophesy,—your old men, shall dream, dreams, your young men, shall see, visions;

text.NRSV: Even on the male and female slaves, in those days, I will pour out my spirit.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, upon the servants and upon the handmaids—in those days, will I pour out my spirit;

text.NRSV: I will show portents in the heavens and on the earth, blood and fire and columns of smoke.
text.Rotherham: And I will set forth wonders in the heavens, and in the earth,—blood, and fire, and columns of smoke:

text.NRSV: The sun shall be turned to darkness, and the moon to blood, before the great and terrible day of the Lord comes.
text.Rotherham: The sun, shall be turned into darkness, and the moon into blood,—before the coming of the great and awful day of Yahweh.

text.NRSV: Then everyone who calls on the name of the Lord shall be saved; for in Mount Zion and in Jerusalem there shall be those who escape, as the Lord has said, and among the survivors shall be those whom the Lord calls.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, whosoever, shall call on the name of Yahweh, shall be delivered,—For in Mount Zion, and in Jerusalem, shall be a delivered remnant, just as Yahweh hath said, and among the survivors, whom Yahweh doth call.

text.NRSV: For then, in those days and at that time, when I restore the fortunes of Judah and Jerusalem,
text.Rotherham: For lo! in those days, and at that time,—when I shall bring back the captivity of Judah and Jerusalem,

text.NRSV: I will gather all the nations and bring them down to the valley of Jehoshaphat, and I will enter into judgment with them there, on account of my people and my heritage Israel, whom they have scattered among the nations. They have divided my land,
text.Rotherham: Then will I gather all the nations, and bring them down into the Vale of Jehoshaphat,—and will enter into judgment with them there, concerning my people, and mine inheritance Israel, whom they scattered among the nations, and, my land, they apportioned;

text.NRSV: and cast lots for my people, and traded boys for prostitutes, and sold girls for wine, and drunk it.
text.Rotherham: And, for my people, they cast lots,—and gave a boy for a harlot, and a girl sold they for wine—and drank.

text.NRSV: What are you to me, O Tyre and Sidon, and all the regions of Philistia? Are you paying me back for something? If you are paying me back, I will turn your deeds back upon your own heads swiftly and speedily.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, what have, ye, to do with me, O Tyre and Zidon, and all the circuit of Palestine? A recompense, are ye paying back unto me? But, though ye should make a recompense unto me, swiftly, speedily, would I return your recompense upon your own head.

text.NRSV: For you have taken my silver and my gold, and have carried my rich treasures into your temples.
text.Rotherham: Because, my silver and my gold, ye took away,—and, my richly beautiful things, carried ye into your temples;

text.NRSV: You have sold the people of Judah and Jerusalem to the Greeks, removing them far from their own border.
text.Rotherham: And, the sons of Judah and the sons of Jerusalem, ye sold to the sons of Greece,—that they might be far removed from their own boundary.

text.NRSV: But now I will rouse them to leave the places to which you have sold them, and I will turn your deeds back upon your own heads.
text.Rotherham: Behold me! rousing them up out of the place whither ye sold them,—So will I bring back your dealing upon your own head.

text.NRSV: I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the people of Judah, and they will sell them to the Sabeans, to a nation far away; for the Lord has spoken.
text.Rotherham: And I will sell your sons and your daughters into the hand of the sons of Judah, and they will sell them to Sabeans, unto a nation afar off,—for, Yahweh, hath spoken.

text.NRSV: Proclaim this among the nations: Prepare war, stir up the mighty; let all the warriors draw near, let them come up.
text.Rotherham: Proclaim ye this, among the nations, Hallow a war, rouse the mighty ones, let them draw near, come up, all the men of war!

text.NRSV: Beat your plowshares into swords, and your pruning hooks into spears; let the weak say, “I am a warrior.”
text.Rotherham: Beat your plough-shares into swords, and your pruning-hooks into spears,—as for the weak, let him say, mighty, I am.

text.NRSV: Hurry and come, all you nations from all around, come together there. Bring down your warriors, O Lord.
text.Rotherham: Give help—and come in, all ye nations on every side, and gather yourselves together,—Thither, bring down, O Yahweh, thy mighty ones!

text.NRSV: Let the nations rouse themselves, and come up to the valley of Jehoshaphat; for there I will sit to judge all the neighboring nations.
text.Rotherham: Let the nations, be roused, and come up, into the Vale of Jehoshaphat,—for, there, will I sit to judge all the nations, on every side.

text.NRSV: Put in the sickle, for the harvest is ripe. Go in, tread, for the wine press is full. The vats overflow, for their wickedness is great.
text.Rotherham: Thrust ye in the vintage knife, for, grown ripe, is the vintage,—Go in, tread down, for full is the winepress, flow over do the vats, for abundant is their wickedness.

text.NRSV: Multitudes, multitudes, in the valley of decision! For the day of the Lord is near in the valley of decision.
text.Rotherham: Multitudes, multitudes, in the vale of strict decision,—for near is the day of Yahweh, in the vale of strict decision.

text.NRSV: The sun and the moon are darkened, and the stars withdraw their shining.
text.Rotherham: The sun and the moon, have become dark,—and, the stars, have withdrawn their shining,

text.NRSV: The Lord roars from Zion, and utters his voice from Jerusalem, and the heavens and the earth shake. But the Lord is a refuge for his people, a stronghold for the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, out of Zion, will roar, and, out of Jerusalem, will utter his voice, and the heavens and the earth, shall tremble,—but, Yahweh, shall be a shelter to his people, and a refuge to the sons of Israel.

text.NRSV: So you shall know that I, the Lord your God, dwell in Zion, my holy mountain. And Jerusalem shall be holy, and strangers shall never again pass through it.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye know that, I, Yahweh, am your God, making my habitation in Zion my holy mountain,—So shall, Jerusalem, be, holy, and, foreigners, shall pass through her no more.

text.NRSV: In that day the mountains shall drip sweet wine, the hills shall flow with milk, and all the stream beds of Judah shall flow with water; a fountain shall come forth from the house of the Lord and water the Wadi Shittim.
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the mountains shall drip sweet wine, and the hills shall flow down with milk, and, all the channels of Judah, shall flow down with waters,—and, a spring, out of the house of Yahweh, shall come forth, and shall water the torrent-valley of the acacias.

text.NRSV: Egypt shall become a desolation and Edom a desolate wilderness, because of the violence done to the people of Judah, in whose land they have shed innocent blood.
text.Rotherham: Egypt, to a desolation, shall be turned, and, Edom, to a desert most desolate, shall be changed,—because of the violence done to the sons of Judah, in that they shed innocent blood in their land.

text.NRSV: But Judah shall be inhabited forever, and Jerusalem to all generations.
text.Rotherham: But, Judah, age-abidingly, shall remain, and, Jerusalem, to generation after generation.

text.NRSV: I will avenge their blood, and I will not clear the guilty, for the Lord dwells in Zion.
text.Rotherham: And I will free from their blood-guiltiness them whom I had not freed,—for, Yahweh, is about to make his habitation in Zion.
